source_code:
# source: https://github.com/jupyter/nbconvert/issues/1559
# repo: jupyter/nbconvert
# title: Template directories not created with world-readable permissions
# 
# I'm using Jypyter notebooks in a multi-user environment, with the anaconda distribution maintained by a non-root user (**user A**). When **user A** first uses a Jupyter notebook, a new `share/jupyter/nbconvert/templates/html` directory is created that is only readable by that user. That causes subsequent use by another user (**user B**) to fail. The suggested fixes are either/both:
# 1. When subdirectories are created in `share/jupyter/nbconvert/templates`, make sure they're world readable (i.e. `go+rx`)
# 2. When a permissions denied error is encountered when reading `nbconvert` settings, don't throw an uncaught exception, especially when user-specific settings can be accessed in the home directory.
# This issue appears related to several other issues (#1167 and #1430). In issue #1167, M

expected_fix_hint: fix 2. However, given that the permissions issue appears to be created by `nbconvert` itself, I think `nbconvert` should be more forgiving. I think there is no reason not to implement fix 1.
